trajectory_summary: This call never connected. The transcript captures only the carrier's automated voicemail greeting — no rep speech, no customer voice, no interaction of any kind. There is no trajectory to read, no energy built or leaked, and no coaching signal derivable from the call itself. The call is a dial-to-voicemail event, not a setter call.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is 'unknown,' which is technically accurate but undersells the issue. This call should be tagged 'no_contact_voicemail' — a distinct outcome class from an attempted call that reached a live customer and failed to convert. Lumping it with 'unknown' obscures the dial-to-connect ratio in the rep's pipeline metrics. If Ion's CRM has a voicemail disposition code, this call belongs there, not in the general unknown bucket.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Voicemail protocol gap — codex and floor process
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available.
primary_coaching_focus.why: There is no rep behavior to coach from this call. The only actionable output is a process question: Ion has no documented voicemail protocol in the codex, and this call entered the analysis queue without being filtered as a no-contact event. The manager's Tuesday action is to raise the voicemail protocol gap with Kenny (what should reps do on a voicemail connect?) and to check whether the transcript pipeline is correctly tagging and routing no-contact dials before they consume coaching review time.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message.
key_moments.stewart_read: The call reached voicemail. The codex has no voicemail protocol section — there is no defined guidance on whether the rep should leave a message, what that message should contain, or how this dial should be dispositioned in the CRM. This is a codex gap, not a rep failure. Strategic-vs-reactive distinction is not applicable: there is no rep behavior to evaluate.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available.
key_moments.stewart_read: A voicemail-connect on a paid lead is a different operational problem than on an organic lead. On a paid lead, Ion has already spent money acquiring this contact — a no-answer without a follow-up protocol is a direct cost. On an organic lead, the stakes are lower. Lead type is unknown for this call, so the severity of the miss cannot be quantified. The pipeline infrastructure question — how are voicemail dials tracked, retried, and dispositioned — is the real issue surfaced here.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
key_moments.stewart_read: A voicemail-connect produces zero sits and zero setter compensation regardless of where the rep sits in their stair-step tier. The compensation model (paid on sits, not dials) means this call has no direct paycheck impact — but it does consume dial time that could have been spent on a live connect. The concern is not this single call; it is whether voicemail-connect calls are being tracked separately from live-connect calls in the rep's activity metrics. If they are not, the rep's 'dials' number is inflated relative to their actual contact opportunities.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available.
key_moments.stewart_read: This transcript was submitted for trajectory analysis but contains no analyzable call content. The pipeline QA question — why a voicemail-only recording is entering the analysis queue — is outside the codex's scope entirely. The codex covers setter behavior on live calls; it has no section governing transcript ingestion, call tagging, or CRM disposition rules. Flagging this as an infrastructure signal, not a coaching signal.
